name: German
blurb: Standard German, the German of Germany. Corlang teaches the language the Goethe-Zertifikat actually tests: the four cases and how they drive every article and ending, verb-second and the sentence bracket that throws the verb to the end, and the everyday register you need at the Amt, at work and with neighbours. B1 is the finish line because B1 is what citizenship and the settlement permit require.
paretoSummary: 80% of everyday German comes from a small core: the ~700 most frequent words, the present of sein, haben and werden plus regular and the common strong verbs, the four cases carried by der/die/das and ein/eine, verb-second in main clauses and verb-last after weil, dass and wenn, the sentence bracket that parks the second verb at the end, the perfect with haben or sein for everything you did, and the modal verbs that let you say what you must, can and want. Learn every noun with its article, since gender decides the case endings, and learn the case a preposition governs at the same moment you learn the preposition. Master this 20% and daily life in Germany opens up.
